profile.behavior: Solitary, patient stalker of small aquatic prey; typically stands motionless or moves slowly at the water’s edge, with the neck drawn in, then strikes with its bill.
field_marks: Small, compact, stocky heron with a thick neck often held tightly retracted.
Deep green to blue-green gloss on the back and crown; may look nearly black in dull light.
Rich chestnut neck and breast, with a dark cap that may form a short crest.
Dark gray rounded wings; juvenile birds are browner with pale neck streaking and pale wing spots.
Orange-yellow legs and a short, square-tipped tail.
profile.habitat: Vegetated wetland edges, including ponds, marshes, swamps, streams, rivers, reservoirs, and estuaries; especially where trees, shrubs, or tall emergent vegetation provide cover.

sources.title: Green Heron Identification — All About Birds, Cornell Lab of Ornithology

findings: Verified identity: adult Green Heron (Butorides virescens), family Ardeidae.

All visible plate text matches the verified measurements, behavior, habitat, and adult/juvenile field marks. The candidate’s chestnut neck and breast, dark crested crown, deep green-blue back, dark rounded wings, long straight bill, orange-yellow legs, and short tail agree with the supplied photos and sources.
The primary full-body illustration has one head, one beak, two wings, two legs, and one tail; the separate head and wing are clearly labeled anatomical studies, not extra anatomy on the bird.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears on the plate.
No material species, anatomy, composition, or text errors found.
